speaker: KING
text: How bloodily the sun begins to peer

speaker: KING
text: Above yon bulky hill. The day looks pale

speaker: KING
text: At his distemp’rature.

speaker: PRINCE
text: The southern wind

speaker: PRINCE
text: Doth play the trumpet to his purposes,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And by his hollow whistling in the leaves

speaker: PRINCE
text: Foretells a tempest and a blust’ring day.

speaker: KING
text: Then with the losers let it sympathize,

speaker: KING
text: For nothing can seem foul to those that win.

speaker: KING
text: How now, my Lord of Worcester? ’Tis not well

speaker: KING
text: That you and I should meet upon such terms

speaker: KING
text: As now we meet. You have deceived our trust

speaker: KING
text: And made us doff our easy robes of peace

speaker: KING
text: To crush our old limbs in ungentle steel.

speaker: KING
text: This is not well, my lord; this is not well.

speaker: KING
text: What say you to it? Will you again unknit

speaker: KING
text: This churlish knot of all-abhorrèd war

speaker: KING
text: And move in that obedient orb again

speaker: KING
text: Where you did give a fair and natural light,

speaker: KING
text: And be no more an exhaled meteor,

speaker: KING
text: A prodigy of fear, and a portent

speaker: KING
text: Of broachèd mischief to the unborn times?

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Hear me, my liege:

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For mine own part I could be well content

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To entertain the lag end of my life

speaker: WORCESTER
text: With quiet hours. For I protest

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I have not sought the day of this dislike.

speaker: KING
text: You have not sought it. How comes it then?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Rebellion lay in his way, and he found it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Peace, chewet, peace.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It pleased your Majesty to turn your looks

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Of favor from myself and all our house;

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And yet I must remember you, my lord,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: We were the first and dearest of your friends.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For you my staff of office did I break

speaker: WORCESTER
text: In Richard’s time, and posted day and night

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To meet you on the way and kiss your hand

speaker: WORCESTER
text: When yet you were in place and in account

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Nothing so strong and fortunate as I.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It was myself, my brother, and his son

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That brought you home and boldly did outdare

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The dangers of the time. You swore to us,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And you did swear that oath at Doncaster,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That you did nothing purpose ’gainst the state,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Nor claim no further than your new-fall’n right,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The seat of Gaunt, dukedom of Lancaster.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To this we swore our aid. But in short space

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It rained down fortune show’ring on your head,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And such a flood of greatness fell on you—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: What with our help, what with the absent king,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: What with the injuries of a wanton time,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The seeming sufferances that you had borne,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And the contrarious winds that held the King

speaker: WORCESTER
text: So long in his unlucky Irish wars

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That all in England did repute him dead—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And from this swarm of fair advantages

speaker: WORCESTER
text: You took occasion to be quickly wooed

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To gripe the general sway into your hand,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Forgot your oath to us at Doncaster;

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And being fed by us, you used us so

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As that ungentle gull, the cuckoo’s bird,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Useth the sparrow—did oppress our nest,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Grew by our feeding to so great a bulk

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That even our love durst not come near your sight

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For fear of swallowing; but with nimble wing

speaker: WORCESTER
text: We were enforced for safety sake to fly

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Out of your sight and raise this present head,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Whereby we stand opposèd by such means

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As you yourself have forged against yourself

speaker: WORCESTER
text: By unkind usage, dangerous countenance,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And violation of all faith and troth

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Sworn to us in your younger enterprise.

speaker: KING
text: These things indeed you have articulate,

speaker: KING
text: Proclaimed at market crosses, read in churches,

speaker: KING
text: To face the garment of rebellion

speaker: KING
text: With some fine color that may please the eye

speaker: KING
text: Of fickle changelings and poor discontents,

speaker: KING
text: Which gape and rub the elbow at the news

speaker: KING
text: Of hurlyburly innovation.

speaker: KING
text: And never yet did insurrection want

speaker: KING
text: Such water colors to impaint his cause,

speaker: KING
text: Nor moody beggars starving for a time

speaker: KING
text: Of pellmell havoc and confusion.

speaker: PRINCE
text: In both your armies there is many a soul

speaker: PRINCE
text: Shall pay full dearly for this encounter

speaker: PRINCE
text: If once they join in trial. Tell your nephew,

speaker: PRINCE
text: The Prince of Wales doth join with all the world

speaker: PRINCE
text: In praise of Henry Percy. By my hopes,

speaker: PRINCE
text: This present enterprise set off his head,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I do not think a braver gentleman,

speaker: PRINCE
text: More active-valiant, or more valiant-young,

speaker: PRINCE
text: More daring or more bold, is now alive

speaker: PRINCE
text: To grace this latter age with noble deeds.

speaker: PRINCE
text: For my part, I may speak it to my shame,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I have a truant been to chivalry,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And so I hear he doth account me too.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yet this before my father’s majesty:

speaker: PRINCE
text: I am content that he shall take the odds

speaker: PRINCE
text: Of his great name and estimation,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And will, to save the blood on either side,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Try fortune with him in a single fight.

speaker: KING
text: And, Prince of Wales, so dare we venture thee,

speaker: KING
text: Albeit considerations infinite

speaker: KING
text: Do make against it.—No, good Worcester, no.

speaker: KING
text: We love our people well, even those we love

speaker: KING
text: That are misled upon your cousin’s part.

speaker: KING
text: And, will they take the offer of our grace,

speaker: KING
text: Both he and they and you, yea, every man

speaker: KING
text: Shall be my friend again, and I’ll be his.

speaker: KING
text: So tell your cousin, and bring me word

speaker: KING
text: What he will do. But if he will not yield,

speaker: KING
text: Rebuke and dread correction wait on us,

speaker: KING
text: And they shall do their office. So begone.

speaker: KING
text: We will not now be troubled with reply.

speaker: KING
text: We offer fair. Take it advisedly.

speaker: PRINCE
text: It will not be accepted, on my life.

speaker: PRINCE
text: The Douglas and the Hotspur both together

speaker: PRINCE
text: Are confident against the world in arms.

speaker: KING
text: Hence, therefore, every leader to his charge,

speaker: KING
text: For on their answer will we set on them,

speaker: KING
text: And God befriend us as our cause is just.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Hal, if thou see me down in the battle and

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: bestride me, so; ’tis a point of friendship.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Nothing but a colossus can do thee that friendship.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Say thy prayers, and farewell.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I would ’twere bedtime, Hal, and all well.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Why, thou owest God a death.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: ’Tis not due yet. I would be loath to pay Him

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: before His day. What need I be so forward with

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Him that calls not on me? Well, ’tis no matter.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Honor pricks me on. Yea, but how if honor prick me

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: off when I come on? How then? Can honor set to a

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: leg? No. Or an arm? No. Or take away the grief of a

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: wound? No. Honor hath no skill in surgery, then?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: No. What is honor? A word. What is in that word

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: “honor”? What is that “honor”? Air. A trim reckoning.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Who hath it? He that died o’ Wednesday. Doth

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: he feel it? No. Doth he hear it? No. ’Tis insensible,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: then? Yea, to the dead. But will it not live with the

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: living? No. Why? Detraction will not suffer it. Therefore,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I’ll none of it. Honor is a mere scutcheon. And

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: so ends my catechism.
